fields.treatmentsPlaceholder: TQ right arm 14:32, NPA, chest seal…
fields.operationNotePlaceholder: Evac coordination, intervention summary, radio frequency…

M.title: MASSIVE BLEEDING
M.subtitle: Massive Bleeding
M.doctrine: // DOCTRINE: ~90% of preventable combat deaths are extremity hemorrhage. Under fire (CUF), apply a high-and-tight tourniquet in the first 60 seconds. Record TQ time on the card in TFC.

A.title: AIRWAY
A.subtitle: Airway
A.doctrine: // DOCTRINE: In an unconscious casualty the tongue can obstruct the airway. If breathing, insert an NPA via the right or left naris. If the airway cannot be opened, plan surgical airway (Cric).

R.title: RESPIRATION
R.subtitle: Respiration
R.doctrine: // DOCTRINE: Apply a vented occlusive chest seal to open chest wounds. If progressive respiratory distress, unilateral chest rise, and hypotension develop, perform needle decompression (NDC) at the 2nd or 5th intercostal space.

C.title: CIRCULATION
C.subtitle: Circulation
C.doctrine: // DOCTRINE: With suspected internal bleeding or shock, administer tranexamic acid (TXA) within the first 3 hours to support clotting. Monitor pulse via the radial artery.

H.title: HYPOTHERMIA & HEAD
H.subtitle: Hypothermia & Head
H.doctrine: // DOCTRINE: Blood loss drives hypothermia, which impairs clotting (lethal triad). Insulate the casualty from the ground and wrap with a thermal blanket.

M.definition: Massive Bleeding — primary cause of preventable death. Stop hemorrhage within the first 60 seconds under CUF.
protocols: High & Tight TQ: highest practicable point on the limb, tight (2–3 inches above the wound).
Record TQ time on DD-1380; re-evaluate every 2 hours.
Junctional / truncal bleeding: hemostatic gauze + pressure bandage when TQ is not feasible.
Priority off the X: single intervention = hemorrhage control.

A.definition: Airway — tongue/obstruction risk with altered consciousness. If breathing → NPA; if not → advanced airway.
protocols: Conscious + breathing → position, aspiration check.
Unconscious + breathing → NPA (right/left naris).
Complete obstruction / no breathing → chin-lift, Cric plan (TFC).
Recovery position: stable lateral, prevent aspiration.

R.definition: Respiration — penetrating chest wound and tension pneumothorax management.
protocols: Vented chest seal: occlusive seal on open chest wound.
NDC: 2nd or 5th intercostal, mid-clavicular — 10G/14G.
Record respiratory rate, SpO2 (if available), unilateral breath sounds.
If bilateral decompression needed, assess both sides.

C.definition: Circulation — shock, internal bleeding, TXA and fluid protocols.
protocols: TXA: 1g IV/IO within first 3 hours (CoTCCC).
IV/IO access; prefer whole blood when available.
Radial pulse: present/absent — perfusion indicator.
Reassess and return to bleeding step (M again) as needed.

H.definition: Hypothermia & Head Injury — lethal triad and neuro protection.
protocols: Hypothermia prevention: thermal wrap, insulate from ground.
Record AVPU and pupils — TBI suspicion.
Head injury: higher evac priority; mind C-spine.
Active warming if available; remove wet clothing.

sim.radioSubtitleCasevac: CASEVAC MIST · HOT ZONE · STRESS WINDOW {{sec}} SEC
sim.failureTitleMedevac: ⚠️ RADIO REPORT ERROR / HELICOPTER LAUNCH DENIED
sim.failureTitleCasevac: ⚠️ RADIO REPORT ERROR / CASEVAC EVACUATION DENIED
sim.generalErrorMedevac: • [GENERAL ERROR]: 9-LINE report below tactical threshold — detailed line audit incomplete.
sim.generalErrorCasevac: • [GENERAL ERROR]: MIST report below tactical threshold — detailed line audit incomplete.
sim.orsEffect: • [ORS EFFECT / TCCC BELOW THRESHOLD] Success percent recorded below 40% — Operational Readiness Score (ORS) −{{penalty}} points (orsEngine · ERROR_CODE: TCCC BELOW THRESHOLD · <40% SESSION)
sim.successCasevac: ⚡ MIST REPORT SUCCESS / CASEVAC ASSET INBOUND

briefing.casevacTitle: [ 📑 PRE-MISSION DOCTRINE BRIEFING: CASEVAC · MIST PROTOCOL ]
briefing.medevacWhy45: • WHY 45 SECONDS? Every extra second on the radio means a bleeding casualty closer to death and a higher chance of team location compromise.
briefing.medevacGolden: • 1. GOLDEN HOUR RULE: For severe trauma, the MEDEVAC call must clear cleanly in the first 90 seconds. The final 45 seconds are reserved to complete a NATO-standard 9-LINE over the radio.
briefing.medevacEw: • 2. ELECTRONIC WARFARE & DF: When continuous talk exceeds 45 seconds, enemy Direction Finding can triangulate your position and put artillery on your grid.
briefing.medevacStress: • 3. STRESS LOCK ANALYSIS: Keeping composure under fire while entering MGRS and casualty counts is a true operator reflex. When time expires you take a radio COLD HIT and ORS is penalized −14.
briefing.hat1Body: • LINE 1 (COORDINATE FLEXIBILITY): Smart parse mode is enabled. Enter plain MGRS (36S TH 1234 5678) or lat/lon (39.7761, 30.5211). Dots, commas, and spaces are tolerated.
briefing.hat3A: • A - URGENT: Will die if not evacuated within ~2 hours (massive hemorrhage, airway obstruction).
briefing.hat3B: • B - URGENT-SURGE: Life-threatening but temporarily stabilized (e.g. TQ).
briefing.hat3C: • C - PRIORITY: Open fractures / injuries with lower immediate life/limb risk.
briefing.hat3D: • D - ROUTINE: Minor casualties who can wait up to 24 hours.
briefing.hat45Body: Equipment is multi-select! You may need both HOIST and LITTER. Total Litter + Ambulatory on Line 5 must exactly match total casualties on Line 3!
briefing.casevacWhy30: • WHY 30 SECONDS? CASEVAC is hot-zone work; radio time is shorter than 9-Line MEDEVAC. Every extra second raises ambush risk for vehicle and team.
briefing.casevacMist: • MIST REPORT: M (Metric/injury type), I (Injury/anatomy), S (Signs/vitals), T (Treatment) — four letters in one breath.
briefing.casevacTq: • TQ + SHOCK CONSISTENCY: If a tourniquet was applied, selecting «No Shock Signs» is a clinical contradiction; the report is rejected.

lines.mistTqWarning: [ MIST WARNING ] TQ + NO-SHOCK CONFLICT — REJECTION RISK
lines.hat1ZeroSpecial: ⚠ ZERO SPECIAL CHARACTERS: Do not use degree (°), minute ('), or second (") symbols. Enter numbers separated by spaces only — e.g. 38 40 25.92 26 45 29.44 — the system converts automatically.
lines.hat1BlocError: UNSEPARATED DIGIT BLOC — SEPARATE WITH SPACES: 38 40 25.92 26 45 29.44
lines.hat1Invalid: INVALID COORDINATE — e.g. '38 40 25.92 26 45 29.44' (6 NUMBERS, SPACED) OR MGRS
lines.hat4Hint: SELECT MULTIPLE ITEMS · e.g. HOIST + EXTRACTION

reject.timeoutMedevac: • [ERROR_CODE: SIGNAL_COMPROMISE] Talk time exceeded 45 seconds! Enemy Direction Finding fixed your position; artillery is inbound on your grid.
reject.timeoutCasevac: • [ERROR_CODE: EVAC_AMBUSH] Radio time exceeded 30 seconds! CASEVAC asset was ambushed in the hot zone — evacuation failed!
reject.hat1Empty: • [🚨 CRITICAL ERROR - NO ROUTE]: LINE 1 (PICKUP COORDINATE) LEFT EMPTY! Pilots cannot spin rotors without a clear geographic target (MGRS/GPS) — helicopter stays in the hangar.
reject.hat1Invalid: • [LINE 1 ERROR]: PICKUP COORDINATE INVALID FORMAT! Enter MGRS (e.g. 36S TH 1234 5678) or GPS (lat, lon) — helicopter cannot launch without a route.
reject.hat1Guide: • [LINE 1 GUIDE]: Strip special symbols and enter numbers with spaces only. Example: '38 40 25.92 26 45 29.44' — the system converts automatically.
reject.hat1Bloc: • [🚨 COORDINATE BLIND SPOT]: YOU ENTERED DIGITS WITHOUT SPACES! Degree/minute/second digit lengths vary by geography, so the system cannot safely split lat/lon. Separate numbers with spaces. Example: '38 40 25.92 26 45 29.44'
reject.hat3Zero: • [LINE 3 ERROR]: Precedence counts (A/B/C/D) missing or total zero — triage data incomplete.
reject.hat5Zero: • [LINE 5 ERROR]: Litter or Ambulatory counts missing — logistics capacity not reported.
reject.hat3Hat5Missing: • [🚨 REACTION ERROR - MISSING MANIFEST]: LINE 3 TOTAL {{hat3}} ≠ LINE 5 TOTAL {{hat5}} · {{missing}} CASUALTY TRANSPORT TYPE(S) MISSING — PILOT DENIED LAUNCH!
reject.hat3Hat5Excess: • [🚨 REACTION ERROR - GHOST PASSENGER]: LINE 3 TOTAL {{hat3}} ≠ LINE 5 TOTAL {{hat5}} · {{excess}} EXTRA PASSENGER(S) — LITTER/AMBULATORY MISMATCH, LAUNCH DENIED!
reject.hat2Empty: • [LINE 2 ERROR]: Radio frequency / callsign empty — MEDEVAC element cannot raise you on net.
reject.hat4Empty: • [LINE 4 ERROR]: No special equipment selected — mark at least one item (HOIST, LITTER, EXTRACTION, etc.).
reject.hat6Empty: • [LINE 6 ERROR]: Pickup security status (N/P/E/X) not selected — pilot cannot build a risk profile.
reject.hat7Empty: • [LINE 7 ERROR]: LZ marking method not selected — recovery team cannot visually confirm the zone.
reject.hat8Empty: • [LINE 8 ERROR]: Patient nationality/status not selected — legal evacuation protocol cannot start.
reject.hat9Empty: • [LINE 9 ERROR]: CBRN threat or terrain not reported — helicopter landing profile cannot be set.
reject.conflictUnmarked: • [LINE 6 & LINE 7 CONFLICT]: {{label}} — marking is mandatory on a secure LZ.
reject.conflictCbrn: • [LINE 7 & LINE 9 CONFLICT]: {{label}} — marking required under CBRN threat.
reject.conflictHotLz: • [LINE 4 & LINE 6 CONFLICT]: {{label}} — hot LZ requires extraction/hoist gear.
reject.conflictUrgent: • [LINE 3, LINE 4 & LINE 9 CONFLICT]: {{label}} — urgent case + terrain obstacles require equipment.
reject.casevacCount: • [MIST ERROR · CASUALTY COUNT]: Total casualty count missing — CASEVAC capacity cannot be calculated.
reject.casevacMetric: • [M — METRIC ERROR]: Injury type (GSW/Shrapnel/Amputation/Burn) not selected.
reject.casevacInjury: • [I — INJURY ERROR]: Injury site/anatomy (Head/Chest/Abdomen/Extremity) not reported.
reject.casevacTreatment: • [T — TREATMENT ERROR]: Intervention (tourniquet, seal, airway, etc.) not marked.
reject.casevacCallsign: • [CASEVAC CALLSIGN ERROR]: Hot-zone callsign / frequency empty — asset cannot be directed.
reject.casevacTqShock: • [MIST TACTICAL WARNING · T+S]: Tourniquet applied but «NO SHOCK SIGNS» selected — clinical contradiction; report rejected.
reject.casevacMorphine: • [MIST TACTICAL WARNING · T+S]: Morphine reported with shock signs present — protocol violation risk.
reject.overtime: • [CRITICAL_DELAY / 🚨 CRITICAL DELAY]: YOU EXCEEDED THE RADIO PROTOCOL LIMIT BY +{{sec}} SECONDS! ENEMY ELEMENTS COMPLETED DIRECTION FINDING AND AMBUSHED THE EVACUATION ASSET.
reject.failureTimeoutMedevac: TIMEOUT · TRANSMISSION TIME EXCEEDED · 45 SEC · +{{sec}} SEC DELAY
reject.failureTimeoutCasevac: TIMEOUT · TRANSMISSION TIME EXCEEDED · 30 SEC · +{{sec}} SEC DELAY

history.emptyHint: COMPLETE A MEDEVAC 9-LINE OR CASEVAC MIST SESSION
history.title: Simulation History · {{count}} record(s)
history.successDebrief: ⚡ [ PROTOCOL VALIDATED ]: RADIO REPORT AND LOGISTICS DATA COMPLETE. CLEAN EVACUATION SUCCESS.
history.failDebriefHeading: [ ⚠️ SYSTEM EVACUATION DENIAL REASONS / MISSION CRITICAL ERRORS ]
